question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: filterMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a topic and receive messages beyond the requested endTime because filterMessage() only checks message.getConsensusTimestamp() >= filter.getStartTime() with no upper-bound endTime check, causing the mirror node to export incorrect (out-of-range) records to the subscriber?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: filterMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user set a very early startTime in TopicMessageFilter and receive messages belonging to a different topic because filterMessage() relies solely on message.getTopicId().equals(filter.getTopicId()) without validating the topicId is non-null, potentially causing a NullPointerException that drops messages and results in missing records?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a silent loss of all records by subscribing at a moment when listenerProperties.isEnabled() returns false, causing listen() to return Flux.empty() with no error or notification, resulting in the subscriber receiving zero records with no indication that data was missing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getTopicListener()] Can an unprivileged external user cause an UnsupportedOperationException to be thrown by triggering a listener type that falls into the default switch branch in getTopicListener(), resulting in the subscriber's stream terminating with an error and all subsequent records being missing from the export?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: recordMetric()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a topic message with a consensusTimestamp so large that the division topicMessage.getConsensusTimestamp() / 1000000 overflows or produces an incorrect millisecond value, causing recordMetric() to silently skip the metric (latency <= 0 check) and masking the fact that the message timestamp is incorrect?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: filterMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TopicMessageFilter with a startTime equal to Long.MAX_VALUE, causing filterMessage()'s condition message.getConsensusTimestamp() >= filter.getStartTime() to never be true, resulting in all records being silently filtered out and missing from the export?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that listen() calls getTopicListener() on every invocation, so if the underlying listener type changes between two subscribe calls, the user receives records from a different backend (e.g., REDIS vs POLL) that may have divergent state, causing incorrect or inconsistent records to be exported?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: filterMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user receive duplicate records because filterMessage() has no deduplication logic and the underlying listener (e.g., RedisTopicListener with retry) may re-emit the same TopicMessage after a reconnect, causing incorrect (duplicate) records to be exported to the mirror node subscriber?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: filterMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user receive out-of-order records because filterMessage() applies no sequence number or ordering validation, and the underlying listener may deliver messages out of consensus order after a retry or reconnect, causing incorrect record ordering in the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user cause missing records by subscribing with a limit set in TopicMessageFilter, since filterMessage() does not enforce the limit and the limit enforcement is delegated entirely to the underlying listener, which may not correctly honor it, resulting in either too many or too few records exported?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getTopicListener()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a race condition where getTopicListener() reads listenerProperties.getType() without synchronization, and a concurrent configuration change causes the returned listener to be inconsistent with the filter already applied, resulting in records from the wrong listener backend being exported?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: filterMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user receive records from other topics when using the SHARED_POLL listener type, because SharedPollingTopicListener.getSharedListener() returns a single global Flux for all topics and relies entirely on filterMessage()'s topicId check, which could be bypassed if message.getTopicId() returns an unexpected value, causing cross-topic record leakage?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: recordMetric()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a message with a consensusTimestamp of 0 or negative, causing the latency calculation System.currentTimeMillis() - (topicMessage.getConsensusTimestamp() / 1000000) to produce an astronomically large positive value that is recorded as a valid metric, masking the fact that the message has an invalid timestamp and should not have been exported?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user cause missing records by opening many concurrent subscriptions to the same topic under the REDIS listener type, triggering the computeIfAbsent race condition in RedisTopicListener.topicMessages, where two Flux instances are created for the same topic and one is discarded, causing some subscribers to miss messages published before their Flux was registered?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: filterMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user receive incorrect records by setting startTime to exactly the consensusTimestamp of a message they should not receive, exploiting the >= (greater-than-or-equal) boundary condition in filterMessage(), causing a message at the boundary to be included when the user's intent was to receive only messages strictly after that timestamp?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user cause all records to be missing from their subscription by subscribing when the REDIS listener is in a retry backoff state (Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, interval)), since the shared Flux is cached and new subscribers joining during the backoff window receive no messages until reconnection completes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: filterMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a sequence number gap check in filterMessage() to remain unaware that the underlying listener skipped messages (e.g., due to a Redis reconnect dropping in-flight messages), causing the subscriber to silently receive an incomplete (missing records) export without any error signal?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger incorrect record export by subscribing with a TopicMessageFilter whose startTime is at the current time boundary, and due to clock skew between the mirror node and the consensus node, filterMessage()'s consensusTimestamp >= startTime check excludes messages that were actually produced before the subscription, causing missing records?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getTopicListener()] Can an unprivileged external user cause missing records by repeatedly subscribing and unsubscribing to a topic under the REDIS listener type, triggering the unsubscribe() path that removes the topic from topicMessages map, so that a subsequent subscriber misses messages published between the unsubscription and the new subscription being established?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: filterMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user receive incorrect records because filterMessage() compares topicId using .equals() on EntityId objects, and if the EntityId implementation has a defective equals() method or the topicId encoding differs between the filter and the message (e.g., shard/realm mismatch), messages from the wrong topic pass the filter and are exported?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user cause missing records by subscribing to a topic under the SHARED_POLL listener type, where SharedPollingTopicListener uses a single global lastConsensusTimestamp that advances based on all messages across all topics, causing the poll window to skip messages for a low-traffic topic that were produced between two poll cycles?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: recordMetric()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the consensusLatencyTimer to record incorrect latency values by publishing messages with a future-dated consensusTimestamp, making System.currentTimeMillis() - (consensusTimestamp / 1000000) negative, which is silently dropped by the latency > 0 guard, masking the fact that incorrect timestamps are present in exported records?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect records to be exported by subscribing with a filter that has a null endTime, since filterMessage() never checks endTime, and the underlying POLL listener also does not enforce endTime in its poll() method, resulting in messages beyond the intended time window being delivered?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: filterMessage()] Can an unprivileged external user cause missing records by subscribing with a startTime that is valid at filter creation time but becomes stale by the time filterMessage() is evaluated, since TopicMessageFilter.isValidStartTime() is only checked at construction and not re-validated during streaming, allowing the filter to silently drop messages that arrive after a long delay?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect records to be exported by exploiting the fact that the SHARED_POLL listener's global Flux is initialized with DomainUtils.now() as the starting timestamp, so a subscriber who connects after initialization misses all messages produced between the initialization time and their subscription time?
